hooks.description: WARN: sweep docs/superpowers/ledgers for stale OPEN trunk ledgers (branch merged/deleted) and lying DoD checkboxes; LEDGER_STRICT=block to harden. Reaper for the semantic-debugging skill.

WARN (exit 0): session-start git hygiene — (1) check_branch_freshness: warn if HEAD is behind @{upstream} or base lags local main (fail-open on offline/fetch-error); (2) prune_worktrees: prune stale registrations, auto-remove merged worktrees, flag unmerged/locked ones. GIT_HYGIENE_OFF=1 to silence; GIT_HYGIENE_PRUNE_OFF=1 for warn-only prune.

WARN (tier 1): test-run hygiene — nudges foreground / 2>&1-captured / unscoped-slow pytest|vitest|cargo-test runs toward `uv run poe run_bg <task>` (detaches → .pytest_logs/, tail it) so long tests don't hang and block memory. TEST_HYGIENE_MODE=block to harden; TEST_HYGIENE_OFF=1 to silence.

WARN (tier 1): before `git push`, `glab mr create`, or `gh pr create` — runs check_branch_freshness to warn if HEAD is behind @{upstream}, base lags local main, OR base lags gitlab/main directly (2026-07-14: closed a false-negative where a stale local main masked real divergence from the canonical gitlab remote, causing duplicate work vs. a concurrent session). Fail-open on offline/fetch-error. GIT_HYGIENE_OFF=1 to silence.

BLOCKING (exit 2): memory-hazard guard — blocks workspace-wide cargo (use -p <crate>), raw 46MB .spectrafit_reports/**/{results,audit}.json loads via cat/jq/python-open/Read (use the live API or KB sidecars), and unbounded spc-bench run/poe benchmark (use --reps 1 or the cheap run_audit/inject path). Single-process loads, KB sidecars, cargo -p, du/wc are allowed; MEM_GUARD_OFF=1 bypasses.

BLOCKING (exit 2): guard tests/fixtures/nist_strd/ against accidental edits — verbatim NIST StRD certified data; only update via nist-strd-runner skill + fresh itl.nist.gov fetch (cf. Cycle 16 / Eckerle4, Cycle 16.C/D/E)
Block frontend/backend boundary violations on Edit: no template engines in Python benchmark exporters, no Pydantic/Python in TSX files
Block dict-key contract access in benchmark/test Python files on Edit; require typed Pydantic accessors
Block if/elif == dispatch chains on a discriminator in python/extras + tests Python files on Edit; require match/case
Block @dataclass / NamedTuple in python/benchmark/ Python files on Edit; require Pydantic BaseModel
Soft-freeze frontend/ on Edit: block exported-symbol deletions and table-header removals; additive changes allowed
Block frontend/backend boundary violations on Write: no template engines in Python benchmark exporters, no Pydantic/Python in TSX files
Block dict-key contract access in benchmark/test Python files on Write; require typed Pydantic accessors
Block if/elif == dispatch chains on a discriminator in python/extras + tests Python files on Write; require match/case
Block @dataclass / NamedTuple in python/benchmark/ Python files on Write; require Pydantic BaseModel
Soft-freeze frontend/ on Write: block full rewrites of existing files; new file creation allowed

WARN (tier 1, SERENA_FIRST_MODE=warn): nudge to mcp__serena__find_symbol when Grep pattern looks like a code symbol (fn/struct/impl/class/def + identifier). Flip to block via SERENA_FIRST_MODE=block in tier 2. See .claude/skills/INDEX.yaml § serena_first.

HYBRID thinking-warning (AAG_FIRST_MODE=block here): before an Agent dispatch, EnterPlanMode, or TaskCreate that looks strategy/architecture/governance/physics-shaped, route the planning through mcp__ai-agent-guidelines__<tool> first. Hard-stops (exit 2 → message reaches the model) only on the four highest-leverage lanes: enterprise-strategy, system-design, policy-govern, physics-analysis. Other lanes stay warn-mode (decorative). Suppressed when scope is clearly narrow (rename/typo/lint/single-file). Rationale: warn-mode stderr never reaches the model context, so the previous default (=warn) was a silent no-op for AI behavior.

Non-blocking warn (exit 0): run `cargo check --workspace --quiet` after editing a Rust file under crates/**/src/ to catch cross-crate breakage at edit time (~3 s warm cache) rather than at maturin develop / pytest time (cf. Cycle 21 / FitResultSpec)

Non-blocking warn (exit 0): compare new ADR headers added in this edit against new topic-index lines; warn with bucket suggestions when an ADR lacks an index entry (cf. Cycle 7 topic-index, 12+ un-indexed ADRs since Cycle 16)
Non-blocking reminder (exit 0) to regenerate bench.schema.json + web/src/contract.ts after editing python/benchmark/contract.py
Non-blocking warn (exit 0) when Python ModelType and Rust ModelTypeStr drift (a member on one side without a matching variant on the other)

hooks.command: grep -n 'pub ' "${tool_input.file_path}" | grep -v '///' | grep -v 'pub(' | grep -v '^--$' | head -10 || true
hooks.description: Surface pub items without leading doc-comment in an edited .rs file (informational, non-blocking)

hooks.prompt: Schema file edited. Verify serde/Pydantic alias parity and add a round-trip test if fields were added or renamed. Use the python-stream skill (it absorbs the schemas specialist) for a full audit.
pytest run completed. If there were failures, use the python-stream skill (TDD dispatch) to classify and route each failure to the correct stream.

hooks.description: Blocks (exit 2) when spectrafit is > 2x slower than lmfit or fit accuracy regresses, by parsing the newest benchmark/quick-validation results.json (script owned by U10). Defensive: the [ -f … ] guard makes it a silent no-op until the script lands, and `exec` preserves the script's exit 2 so the block propagates once it exists.

Non-blocking (exit 0): after a bug/issue-pattern Grep, suggest BPDD-MAP enrichment trio — mcp__github__search_issues + search_code + get_file_contents, context7, WebSearch. DISCOVERY_ENRICHMENT_MODE=off to silence. Complements enforce-serena-first (PreToolUse/symbol patterns).

Conditional DECISIONS.md ADR reminder — exit 2 (feed back) ONLY when the tree has uncommitted changes to a behavioural/architectural surface (crates/spectrafit_core/benchmark/frontend/CI) and DECISIONS.md was not updated; silent (exit 0) on clean/idle turns and doc/skill-only edits. Replaces the unconditional prompt that looped on idle turns.
